mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.02, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate cuts in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered months, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 50% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in new home sales and existing home sales reflect synchronized housing market recovery that amplifies consumer confidence and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive housing signals fail to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during periods of accommodative monetary policy indicate expanding manufacturing capacity without inflationary pressure, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Signal accuracy drops below 45% during easing regimes, or 5-day forward returns show negative correlation with industrial production growth in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with stagnant or rising short-term interest rates creates a negative real yield environment that compresses equity valuations and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward testing over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.01, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive relationship with payroll growth across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP exceeding 0.5% signals economic contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of contraction quarters, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production alongside declining consumer price inflation indicates a supply-side boom that lowers input costs and boosts corporate margins, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns fail to exceed baseline by at least 0.5% in triggered periods, or the joint signal loses predictive power in walk-forward testing over any 3-year window.
